company.name: Gul-e-Arsh Tour & Travels
company.tagline: Where every journey begins with trust and ends with unforgettable memories.
company.description: At Gul-e-Arsh Tour & Travels, we believe that travel is more than just visiting places — it's about creating unforgettable memories that last a lifetime. Based in the heart of Kashmir, we are a passionate team of travel experts dedicated to showcasing the breathtaking beauty of the Valley to the world. From snow-capped mountains to serene lakes, from lush green meadows to ancient Mughal gardens — we curate experiences that go beyond sightseeing. Every trip we design is crafted with care, ensuring comfort, safety, and an authentic connection with the culture and people of Kashmir.
company.mission: Our mission is to provide exceptional travel services with honesty, reliability, and a personal touch. We strive to make Kashmir accessible to every traveler — whether it's a dream honeymoon, a thrilling adventure, or a peaceful family retreat. We are committed to sustainable tourism that respects local culture while delivering world-class hospitality.

contact.address: Dal Gate, Boulevard Road, Srinagar, Kashmir 190001, India

services.title: Customized Tour Packages
services.description: Tailor-made itineraries designed around your preferences, budget, and schedule for a truly personal Kashmir experience.

services.title: Hotel & Houseboat Bookings
services.description: From luxury houseboats on Dal Lake to cozy mountain resorts, we secure the best stays at the best prices.

services.title: Honeymoon & Family Tours
services.description: Romantic getaways and family-friendly adventures crafted to create memories that last forever.

services.title: Adventure & Trekking
services.description: From the Great Lakes trek to skiing in Gulmarg, experience the adrenaline rush of Kashmir's wild landscapes.

services.title: Local Sightseeing
services.description: Discover hidden gems and iconic landmarks with our expert local guides who know every corner of the Valley.

services.title: Airport Transfers & Transport
services.description: Comfortable, reliable transportation from the moment you land to the moment you depart. Stress-free travel guaranteed.

whyChooseUs.title: Local Expertise
whyChooseUs.description: Born and raised in Kashmir, our team knows every hidden trail, secret viewpoint, and best-kept local secret.

whyChooseUs.title: Affordable Pricing
whyChooseUs.description: Premium experiences without the premium price tag. Transparent pricing with no hidden fees or surprises.

whyChooseUs.title: 24/7 Support
whyChooseUs.description: Round-the-clock assistance throughout your trip. One call and we're there — anytime, anywhere in the Valley.

whyChooseUs.title: Professional Team
whyChooseUs.description: Certified guides, experienced drivers, and dedicated trip managers ensure a seamless journey from start to finish.

whyChooseUs.title: Safety First
whyChooseUs.description: Your safety is our top priority. We follow strict safety protocols and maintain fully insured vehicles.

whyChooseUs.title: Sustainable Tourism
whyChooseUs.description: We believe in responsible travel that preserves Kashmir's natural beauty for generations to come.

testimonials.name: Anjali Raghav
testimonials.review: I never thought a family trip could be this relaxing. The team handled everything perfectly — from airport pickup to hotel check-ins. The kids absolutely loved the pony rides in Pahalgam!

testimonials.name: Rishika Verma
testimonials.review: As a solo female traveler, I felt completely safe, supported, and welcomed. The 24/7 support gave me total peace of mind. Kashmir is magical and this team made it even better!

testimonials.name: Falak Imran
testimonials.review: From the first Shikara ride on Dal Lake to the snowy peaks of Gulmarg, every moment felt like a scene from a movie. The guides were incredibly knowledgeable and warm.

testimonials.name: Himanshu Mehta
testimonials.review: Walking through the meadows of Pahalgam felt like a dream. Thank you for showing us the hidden gems away from the tourist crowds. Will definitely come back!

testimonials.name: Priya & Karthik
testimonials.review: Our honeymoon in Kashmir was absolutely perfect! The houseboat stay, the Shikara ride at sunset, the snow in Gulmarg — everything was beautifully planned. A fairytale experience!

testimonials.name: Lyana Thompson
testimonials.review: The guides' energy and local knowledge turned a simple trek into an unforgettable adventure. The Great Lakes Trek was the highlight of my trip to India!

packages.title: Kashmir Paradise Tour
packages.description: Explore the mesmerizing beauty of Kashmir with this all-inclusive tour covering the three jewels of the Valley. From the iconic Dal Lake to the snow-capped peaks of Gulmarg, this package offers the perfect introduction to paradise on earth.
packages.shortDescription: Experience the best of Srinagar, Gulmarg, and Pahalgam in one unforgettable journey through the Valley.
highlights: Shikara ride on Dal Lake at sunset
Gulmarg Gondola — world's highest cable car
Visit to Mughal Gardens — Nishat, Shalimar & Chashme Shahi

itinerary.title: Arrival in Srinagar
itinerary.details: Arrive at Srinagar Airport. Transfer to a traditional houseboat on Dal Lake. Evening Shikara ride watching the sunset paint the water golden. Overnight stay on houseboat.

itinerary.title: Srinagar Mughal Gardens
itinerary.details: Full day exploring the magnificent Mughal Gardens — Nishat Bagh (Garden of Pleasure), Shalimar Bagh (Abode of Love), and Chashme Shahi (Royal Spring). Visit the ancient Shankaracharya Temple for panoramic views. Evening free for local market exploration.

itinerary.title: Srinagar to Gulmarg
itinerary.details: Drive through pine forests to Gulmarg (Meadow of Flowers). Ride the famous Gulmarg Gondola to Phase 1 and Phase 2 for breathtaking mountain views. Optional skiing/snowboarding in winter. Overnight in Gulmarg hotel.

itinerary.title: Gulmarg to Pahalgam
itinerary.details: Scenic drive from Gulmarg to Pahalgam via saffron fields of Pampore. Arrive at the Valley of Shepherds. Explore Betaab Valley (Bollywood filming location) and Aru Valley. Overnight in Pahalgam.

itinerary.title: Pahalgam Exploration
itinerary.details: Full day in Pahalgam — pony ride to Baisaran (Mini Switzerland), visit Chandanwari, and explore the pristine Lidder River. Optional trek to Tarsar Lake viewpoint. Evening bonfire and BBQ dinner.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.details: Leisurely morning breakfast. Transfer back to Srinagar Airport for departure with beautiful memories and a promise to return.

packages.title: Royal Kashmir Honeymoon
packages.description: A romantic escape designed for couples, featuring luxury houseboat stays, candlelit dinners by the lake, private Shikara rides, and the most romantic spots in the Valley. Create memories that will last a lifetime.
packages.shortDescription: A dreamy honeymoon experience featuring luxury stays, romantic Shikara rides, and snow-capped mountain views.
highlights: Private sunset Shikara ride for couples
Gulmarg Gondola ride with mountain views

itinerary.title: Welcome to Paradise
itinerary.details: Arrive at Srinagar Airport. VIP transfer to luxury houseboat on Dal Lake. Welcome drink and flower decoration. Private sunset Shikara ride with Kashmiri Kahwa. Candlelit dinner on the houseboat.

itinerary.title: Romantic Srinagar
itinerary.details: Visit the beautiful Mughal Gardens. Private photography session at Pari Mahal (Palace of Fairies). Evening visit to the floating vegetable market on Dal Lake. Spa session at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Enchanting Gulmarg
itinerary.details: Drive to Gulmarg through scenic pine forests. Ride the Gondola to snow-covered peaks. Horse ride through the meadows. Cozy evening at the mountain resort with bonfire.

itinerary.title: Gulmarg to Pahalgam
itinerary.details: Scenic drive to Pahalgam through the countryside. Check into riverside resort. Private picnic at Betaab Valley. Walk along the Lidder River at sunset.

itinerary.title: Pahalgam Romance
itinerary.details: Pony ride to Baisaran (Mini Switzerland). Visit Aru Valley with packed gourmet lunch. Evening cultural program with Kashmiri music and dance.

itinerary.title: Golden Sonmarg
itinerary.details: Day trip to Sonmarg — the Meadow of Gold. Visit Thajiwas Glacier. Snow activities and photography. Return to Srinagar for farewell dinner at a premium restaurant.

itinerary.title: Fond Farewell
itinerary.details: Leisurely breakfast. Shopping for Kashmiri handicrafts. Transfer to airport with a photo album of your memories.

packages.title: Kashmir Great Lakes Trek
packages.description: Embark on one of India's most stunning treks — the Kashmir Great Lakes Trek. Traverse rugged mountain passes and witness seven turquoise alpine lakes surrounded by untouched meadows and snow-capped peaks.
packages.shortDescription: Trek through seven pristine alpine lakes surrounded by snow-capped peaks — India's most beautiful trek.
highlights: All camping gear and meals provided

itinerary.title: Arrival & Acclimatization
itinerary.details: Arrive in Srinagar. Transfer to Sonamarg. Briefing and gear check. Short acclimatization walk. Overnight in base camp.

itinerary.title: Sonamarg to Nichnai
itinerary.details: Trek from Sonamarg to Nichnai campsite (8 km). Gradual ascent through pine forests. Set up camp near the river. Evening bonfire.

itinerary.title: Nichnai to Vishansar Lake
itinerary.details: Cross Nichnai Pass (13,500 ft). Descend to the stunning turquoise Vishansar Lake. Camp by the lakeshore with panoramic mountain views.

itinerary.title: Vishansar to Gadsar Lake
itinerary.details: Trek past Kishansar Lake. Cross Gadsar Pass (13,750 ft) — the highest point. Descend to Gadsar Lake surrounded by wildflower meadows.

itinerary.title: Gadsar to Satsar Lakes
itinerary.details: Trek to the cluster of seven mountain lakes called Satsar. Camp in one of the most remote and beautiful spots in the Himalayas.

itinerary.title: Satsar to Gangabal Lake
itinerary.details: Descend through meadows to the twin lakes of Gangabal and Nundkol. Stunning views of Mount Harmukh. Camp by the lake.

itinerary.title: Gangabal to Naranag
itinerary.details: Final descent from Gangabal to Naranag through forests. Visit ancient Naranag Temple. Transfer back to Srinagar.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.details: Morning at leisure in Srinagar. Transfer to airport for departure.

packages.title: Classic Family Retreat
packages.description: An easy-paced, family-friendly tour designed to let every family member — from grandparents to toddlers — enjoy the beauty of Kashmir stress-free. Comfortable stays, kid-friendly activities, and zero hassle.
packages.shortDescription: A relaxed, family-friendly Kashmir tour with comfortable stays, pony rides, and zero-hassle sightseeing.
highlights: Pony rides for kids in Pahalgam
Shikara ride suitable for all ages

itinerary.title: Family Welcome
itinerary.details: Arrive at Srinagar Airport. Spacious family vehicle transfer to hotel. Evening Shikara ride on Dal Lake — kids will love spotting the kingfishers! Welcome dinner with Kashmiri Wazwan.

itinerary.title: Garden Day
itinerary.details: Visit Mughal Gardens at a relaxed pace. Tulip Garden visit (seasonal). Interactive craft workshop for kids. Evening free for family time at hotel.

itinerary.title: Snow Day in Gulmarg
itinerary.details: Drive to Gulmarg. Gondola ride (Phase 1 — suitable for families). Snow play activities for kids. Horse riding through the meadows. Overnight in family suite.

itinerary.title: Pahalgam Adventures
itinerary.details: Travel to Pahalgam. Pony rides for the whole family. Visit Betaab Valley for a family picnic. River-side walk along the Lidder. Evening games and bonfire.

itinerary.title: Pahalgam & Back
itinerary.details: Morning visit to Aru Valley. Return to Srinagar. Shopping for souvenirs — papier-mâché, pashmina shawls. Farewell dinner cruise on Dal Lake.

itinerary.title: Happy Goodbye
itinerary.details: Breakfast with scenic views. Transfer to Srinagar Airport with gifts and memories galore.

packages.title: Winter Wonderland Gulmarg
packages.description: Experience Gulmarg at its most magical — blanketed in pristine white snow. This winter-exclusive package includes skiing lessons, snowboarding, igloo building, and the thrill of riding the world's highest cable car above the clouds.
packages.shortDescription: Ski, snowboard, and build igloos in the world's highest ski resort — Gulmarg's winter magic awaits.
highlights: Snow trekking to frozen Alpather Lake
Hot Kashmiri Kahwa by the fireplace

itinerary.title: Arrival in Winter Paradise
itinerary.details: Arrive at Srinagar Airport. Drive through snow-covered landscapes to Gulmarg. Check into heated mountain lodge. Evening snowfall viewing with hot Kahwa. Welcome dinner.

itinerary.title: Ski & Snowboard Day
itinerary.details: Full day of skiing/snowboarding at the Gulmarg Ski Resort. Professional instructor for beginners. Equipment provided. Lunch at the slope-side café. Evening relaxation at lodge.

itinerary.title: Gondola & Snow Trek
itinerary.details: Ride the Gulmarg Gondola to Phase 2 (13,780 ft). Snow trek towards Alpather Lake. Photography session with Himalayan backdrop. Igloo building workshop. Evening bonfire with music.

itinerary.title: Srinagar Winter Charm
itinerary.details: Drive back to Srinagar. Winter Shikara ride on Dal Lake. Visit the snow-dusted Mughal Gardens. Shopping for winter Pashmina. Farewell dinner at traditional Kashmiri restaurant.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.details: Breakfast with mountain views. Transfer to Srinagar Airport.

packages.title: The Ultimate Kashmir Experience
packages.description: The definitive Kashmir experience covering every iconic destination and hidden gem. From the gardens of Srinagar to the glaciers of Sonmarg, from the meadows of Yusmarg to the valleys of Pahalgam — this is Kashmir in its entirety.
packages.shortDescription: The complete Kashmir journey — every iconic destination and hidden gem in one grand tour.

itinerary.title: Grand Arrival
itinerary.details: VIP pickup from Srinagar Airport. Check into luxury hotel. Evening Shikara ride on Dal Lake. Welcome Wazwan dinner.

itinerary.title: Srinagar Heritage
itinerary.details: Full day exploring Srinagar — Mughal Gardens, Hazratbal Shrine, Jama Masjid, and the old city's craft workshops. Evening floating market visit.

itinerary.title: Enchanting Gulmarg
itinerary.details: Drive to Gulmarg. Full Gondola experience. Meadow walks and ATV rides. Overnight in mountain resort.

itinerary.title: Gulmarg to Pahalgam
itinerary.details: Scenic drive via Srinagar to Pahalgam. Stop at saffron fields of Pampore. Evening at Betaab Valley.

itinerary.title: Pahalgam Valleys
itinerary.details: Explore Aru Valley, Baisaran, and Chandanwari. Pony rides and river-side activities. Cultural evening program.

itinerary.title: Hidden Yusmarg
itinerary.details: Day trip to the untouched meadows of Yusmarg. Picnic by the Doodhganga River. Horse riding through wildflower fields. Return to Srinagar.

itinerary.title: Golden Sonmarg
itinerary.details: Drive to Sonmarg — Meadow of Gold. Visit Thajiwas Glacier. Zero Point excursion. Snow activities. Return to Srinagar.

itinerary.title: Doodhpathri Discovery
itinerary.details: Day trip to the Valley of Milk — Doodhpathri. Pristine meadows and gushing streams. Leisure time. Farewell gala dinner.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.details: Morning shopping for Kashmiri treasures. Transfer to airport with a heart full of memories.

locations.name: Srinagar
locations.subtitle: The Heart of the Valley
locations.description: Where the sky paints itself upon the lake and life floats gently on painted boats. Lose yourself in ancient Mughal gardens where emperors once walked, and let the rhythm of the oar become your heartbeat. Srinagar is the summer capital of Jammu & Kashmir and the cultural soul of the entire valley.
locations.longDescription: Srinagar, nestled in the heart of the Kashmir Valley at an altitude of 1,585 metres, is one of India's most romantic and historically rich cities. The city is built around the serene Dal Lake, which has been the defining icon of Kashmir for centuries. Houseboats crafted from walnut wood and intricately carved cedarwood line the shores, offering some of the most unique accommodation experiences in the world. The Mughal gardens — Shalimar Bagh, Nishat Bagh, and Chashme Shahi — are masterpieces of landscape architecture, built during the reign of emperors who called Kashmir paradise on earth. The old city is a labyrinth of narrow lanes filled with artisans crafting papier-mâché, pashmina, and hand-knotted carpets passed down through generations.

whatToSee.title: Dal Lake
whatToSee.description: The iconic lake of Kashmir — sail on a Shikara, visit the floating gardens, and watch the sunrise paint the water gold.

whatToSee.title: Shalimar Bagh
whatToSee.description: Emperor Jehangir's garden of love, featuring tiered terraces, flowing water channels, and ancient chinar trees.

whatToSee.title: Nishat Bagh
whatToSee.description: The Garden of Pleasure — 12 terraced levels cascading down from the Zabarwan mountains to the lakeside.

whatToSee.title: Shankaracharya Temple
whatToSee.description: Perched atop a hill, offering a 360-degree panoramic view of Srinagar, the Dal Lake, and the surrounding mountains.

whatToSee.title: Old City Bazaars
whatToSee.description: Lanes filled with craftsmen making papier-mâché, pashmina shawls, hand-knotted carpets, and silver jewellery.

whatToSee.title: Hazratbal Shrine
whatToSee.description: A revered Muslim shrine on the banks of Dal Lake, known for its white marble architecture and spiritual atmosphere.

locations.name: Gulmarg
locations.subtitle: The Meadow of Flowers
locations.description: A powder-white dream in winter, a floral paradise in summer. Home to the world's highest cable car and Asia's premier ski resort, Gulmarg is where adventure meets serenity amidst towering Himalayan peaks.
locations.longDescription: Gulmarg, meaning 'Meadow of Flowers', sits at an elevation of 2,650 metres and is one of the Himalayas' most spectacular alpine destinations. In winter, it transforms into a pristine white wonderland, hosting Asia's highest and second longest cable car — the Gulmarg Gondola — which carries visitors to a height of 3,980 metres above sea level. The ski slopes here are world-class, attracting skiers from across the globe. In summer and spring, the meadow bursts into colour with wildflowers, making it a paradise for trekkers and nature lovers. The gentle slopes, cool weather, and surrounding peaks make it equally magical across all seasons.

whatToSee.title: Gulmarg Gondola
whatToSee.description: The world's highest operating cable car — ride to Phase 1 at 3,088m or Phase 2 at 3,980m for breathtaking Himalayan panoramas.

whatToSee.title: Ski Resort
whatToSee.description: Asia's premier ski destination with gentle beginner slopes and steep expert runs, fully equipped with rental shops and instructors.

whatToSee.title: Alpather Lake
whatToSee.description: A frozen alpine lake at 3,843 metres, accessible by a thrilling trek through snow — one of Kashmir's most beautiful hidden gems.

whatToSee.title: Bota Pathri
whatToSee.description: A hidden meadow 13 km from Gulmarg carpeted with wildflowers and ringed by snowy peaks — far fewer tourists than Gulmarg itself.

whatToSee.title: Golf Course
whatToSee.description: One of the world's highest golf courses at 2,650m, surrounded by pine forests and mountain views — a round here is unforgettable.

whatToSee.title: Maharani Temple
whatToSee.description: A hilltop Hindu temple with sweeping views of the Gulmarg bowl and the surrounding snow-capped Himalayan range.

locations.name: Pahalgam
locations.subtitle: The Valley of Shepherds
locations.description: Singing pines and roaring rivers — nature's quiet lullaby. Pahalgam enchants with its pristine valleys, pony trails through wildflower meadows, and the crystal-clear waters of the Lidder River winding through town.
locations.longDescription: Pahalgam, the 'Valley of Shepherds', is set at 2,130 metres at the confluence of the streams flowing from Sheshnag Lake and the Lidder River. It was famously used as a Bollywood filming location and continues to enchant visitors with its cinematic beauty. The town itself is a base for trekkers heading to the Amarnath Shrine, and for those seeking to explore the dramatic side valleys of Betaab, Aru, and Chandanwari. Pony rides are a quintessential Pahalgam experience — climbing up to Baisaran, locally called 'Mini Switzerland', for views that feel almost too beautiful to be real.

whatToSee.title: Betaab Valley
whatToSee.description: Named after the hit Bollywood film shot here — a breathtaking valley of green meadows, streams, and towering mountains.

whatToSee.title: Aru Valley
whatToSee.description: A serene side-valley 12 km from Pahalgam — a favourite base for trekkers heading to Kolahoi Glacier and Tarsar Lake.

whatToSee.title: Baisaran (Mini Switzerland)
whatToSee.description: A flat meadow surrounded by dense pine forests and snow-capped peaks — accessible by pony ride from Pahalgam village.

whatToSee.title: Chandanwari
whatToSee.description: The starting point of the annual Amarnath Yatra — a spectacular snow-covered terrain with a famous snowbridge.

whatToSee.title: Lidder River
whatToSee.description: Walk or sit beside this swift glacial river running through the heart of Pahalgam — perfect for trout fishing and river-gazing.

whatToSee.title: Mamaleshwara Temple
whatToSee.description: A small ancient Shiva temple on the banks of the Lidder, one of the oldest temples in Kashmir dating back to the 12th century.

locations.name: Sonmarg
locations.subtitle: The Meadow of Gold
locations.description: Golden meadows embraced by silver glaciers. Sonmarg is the gateway to Ladakh and the starting point for some of Kashmir's most dramatic landscapes — from the Thajiwas Glacier to the turquoise waters of alpine lakes.
locations.longDescription: Sonmarg, or 'Meadow of Gold', earns its poetic name from the golden hues that wash over its vast alpine meadow in autumn. Situated at 2,730 metres, it sits on the Srinagar–Leh highway and serves as the last major town before the dramatic high-altitude passes that lead into Ladakh. The Thajiwas Glacier, a short trek from the main meadow, is a year-round snow destination that delights visitors with snow activities even in late summer. The Sindh River, which originates near Sonmarg, is a favourite for trout fishing, while Vishansar and Krishansar Lakes — part of the Great Lakes Trek — begin their journey from this base.

whatToSee.title: Thajiwas Glacier
whatToSee.description: A stunning glacier accessible by pony or a short trek — snow activities here are possible even in July and August.

whatToSee.title: Zero Point
whatToSee.description: The point where the road ends and only snow begins — a dramatic, icy landscape at over 3,500 metres elevation.

whatToSee.title: Sindh River
whatToSee.description: A sparkling glacial river ideal for trout fishing, riverside picnics, and quiet reflection amidst majestic mountain scenery.

whatToSee.title: Vishansar Lake
whatToSee.description: A stunning turquoise alpine lake at 3,710 metres, reached by trekking through the Great Lakes trail from Sonmarg.

whatToSee.title: Baltal Valley
whatToSee.description: A dramatic valley serving as the base camp for the Amarnath Yatra — breathtaking mountain scenery and a roaring river.

whatToSee.title: Nichnai Pass
whatToSee.description: A high mountain pass above 4,000 metres offering spectacular views — accessible to trekkers on the Great Lakes circuit.

locations.name: Yusmarg
locations.subtitle: The Untouched Paradise
locations.description: Untouched pastures where only the wind speaks. Far from the tourist trail, Yusmarg offers a glimpse of what Kashmir was centuries ago — vast meadows dotted with wildflowers, surrounded by dense deodar forests.
locations.longDescription: Yusmarg, sitting at 2,400 metres, is one of Kashmir's best-kept secrets — a vast, pristine meadow where the tumult of modern tourism feels a world away. The Doodhganga River, its crystal-clear waters milky from glacial melt, flows through the valley creating a scene of impossible beauty. The nearby Charar-i-Sharief shrine, one of Kashmir's most important Sufi pilgrimage sites, adds a spiritual dimension to a visit here. Unlike Gulmarg or Pahalgam, Yusmarg remains genuinely undiscovered — you may share a meadow with nothing but grazing horses and the wind in the pines.

whatToSee.title: Doodhganga River
whatToSee.description: A glacially-fed river of milky blue-white water rushing through the valley floor — one of Kashmir's most photogenic waterways.

whatToSee.title: Charar-i-Sharief
whatToSee.description: A revered Sufi shrine, 13 km away, dedicated to Sheikh Noor-ud-Din Wali — Kashmir's patron saint — set in a hilltop village.

whatToSee.title: Frozen Lakes
whatToSee.description: Several small alpine lakes near Yusmarg remain frozen well into June — magical to walk around in the spring snowmelt season.

whatToSee.title: Nilnag Lake
whatToSee.description: A pristine lake surrounded by dense deodar forest accessible by horse — a serene picnic spot with mountain reflections.

whatToSee.title: Sunset Meadow Views
whatToSee.description: The open meadows offer unobstructed sunset views over the Pir Panjal range — a photographer's dream in golden hour.

whatToSee.title: Village Homestays
whatToSee.description: A rare opportunity to stay in a Kashmiri shepherd's village, sharing authentic cuisine and culture away from hotel tourism.

locations.name: Doodhpathri
locations.subtitle: The Valley of Milk
locations.description: Named after its milky-white streams that cascade through emerald meadows, Doodhpathri is Kashmir's best-kept secret. A hidden paradise of rolling grasslands and pine forests untouched by commercial tourism.
locations.longDescription: Doodhpathri, or 'Valley of Milk', takes its name from the milky white, frothy streams that tumble through its rolling green meadows. Situated at an elevation of 2,730 metres in the Budgam district, it remains one of the least commercialised destinations in Kashmir — a dream for those seeking Kashmir without the crowds. The vast expanse of grassland, framed by dense forests of fir and pine, is a favourite grazing ground for Gujjar and Bakarwal nomadic shepherds. A day trip from Srinagar, Doodhpathri rewards visitors with wildflower carpets in spring and a golden silence broken only by the rush of glacial streams.

whatToSee.title: Glacial Meadows
whatToSee.description: Vast open grasslands at 2,730 metres, carpeted with wildflowers in spring and gold in autumn — completely unspoiled.

whatToSee.title: Milky Streams
whatToSee.description: The namesake frothy white streams fed by glacial meltwater rush through the meadows — perfect for a gentle wade in summer.

whatToSee.title: Tosa Maidan
whatToSee.description: A sprawling high-altitude meadow 15 km further into the mountains — one of Asia's largest alpine meadows, reached only on foot.

whatToSee.title: Pine Forest Trails
whatToSee.description: Dense forests of Himalayan fir and blue pine surround the meadow — peaceful walking trails through ancient woodland.

whatToSee.title: Shepherd Camps
whatToSee.description: Interact with Gujjar nomadic shepherds who bring their flocks here in summer — a window into a vanishing way of life.

whatToSee.title: Stargazing
whatToSee.description: With virtually zero light pollution, Doodhpathri offers some of the most spectacular night sky views anywhere in Kashmir.

faqs.question: Is it safe to travel to Kashmir?
faqs.answer: Yes! Kashmir has seen a significant boost in tourism in recent years and is perfectly safe for travelers. Our team provides 24/7 on-ground support to ensure your complete safety and comfort throughout your trip.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Kashmir?
faqs.answer: Kashmir is beautiful year-round! March–May for tulips and spring blooms, June–August for pleasant summer weather, September–November for stunning autumn colors, and December–February for magical snowfall and skiing.

faqs.question: Can I customize the tour packages?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! All our packages are fully customizable. Tell us your preferences, budget, and dates, and we'll create a tailor-made itinerary just for you.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a Kashmir trip?
faqs.answer: Pack layers! Even in summer, evenings can be cool. In winter, bring heavy woolens, thermals, snow boots, and gloves. We provide a detailed packing list after booking.

faqs.question: Do you provide airport pickup and drop?
faqs.answer: Yes, all our packages include complimentary airport pickup and drop-off at Srinagar Airport (SXR). Our driver will be waiting for you at the arrivals gate.
